title: Unidentified star (Dilonexa system)

A [[Giant star|giant]] [[Color/Legends|blue]]-white '''star''' was situated in the [[Dilonexa system/Legends|Dilonexa system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Twenty-two [[Electromagnetic radiation|radiation]]-seared [[Planet/Legends|planets]] and seventeen [[Water/Legends|ice]]-bound worlds [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Star/Legends|star]] as well as the [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]] [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] [[Dilonexa XXIII]], which was the only world in the [[Star system/Legends|system]] capable of supporting life.

==Description==
[[File:Dilonexa_system.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The star was located in the Dilonexa system.]]
A [[Giant star|gigantic]] [[Color/Legends|blue]]-white [[Star/Legends|star]] that was only identified by a catalog number was located in the [[Dilonexa system/Legends|Dilonexa system]],<ref name="LCFO" /> a part of the [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was situated on the [[Falko Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]], which linked it to the [[Ringneldia system/Legends|Ringneldia]] and [[Ua system]]s.<ref name="TEA" />

On the twenty-two inner [[Planet/Legends|planets]] of the Dilonexa [[Star system/Legends|system]], the [[Electromagnetic radiation|radiation]] from system's star was so intense that a [[Human/Legends|Human's]] unprotected skin would become tanned in a span of [[Microsecond/Legends|microseconds]]. Conversely, the outer seventeen planets were [[Temperature/Legends|frozen]] [[Water/Legends|ice]] worlds. Between those two groups of planets, however, lay [[Dilonexa XXIII]], a large,<ref name="LCFO" /> temperate<ref name="EGtD">''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]]<ref name="LCFO" /> which was the only world in the system that could support life.<ref name="CSWE Dilonexa">{{CSWECite|I|189|Dilonexa XXIII}}</ref>

==History==
Dilonexa XXIII was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] known as [[Dilonexican]]s. In the view of the earliest Dilonexicans, the bountiful natural resources of the planet more than outweighed the extreme conditions of the rest of the star system. When the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]] of the interstellar [[Merchant/Legends|trader]] [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], approached Dilonexa XXIII<ref name="LCFO" /> at some point between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] novel trilogy, including ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', between [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] and [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]].</ref> Calrissian did not bother asking his [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], the [[Class Two multi-phasic robot|droid]] [[Vuffi Raa]], about the catalog number of the planet's sun, which he had forgotten.<ref name="LCFO" />

==Behind the scenes==
The star of the Dilonexa system was first mentioned in ''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'', the [[1983]] second entry in [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] trilogy of novels by [[L. Neil Smith]].<ref name="LCFO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the system, and therefore the star, in grid square U-8.<ref name="TEA" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Lando Calrissian and the Flamewind of Oseon]]'' {{1stm}}
